r : ℝ
hr : r ≥ 0
⊢ HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1))

    apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
    exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  exact h.hasFiniteIntegral

₂

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous and the interval is compact to show finite integral
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      -- Apply the theorem that a continuous function on a compact interval has a finite integral
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;> try norm_num <;>
      (try
        {
          exact h₂
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          exact isCompact_Icc
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          exact isOpen_Icc
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          norm_num at hr ⊢
          <;>
          linarith
        })
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Prove that Φ₄ r is continuous and bounded on [0, 1]
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    -- Use the fact that the interval [0, 1] is compact and Φ₄ r is continuous to show it is bounded
    have h₃ : BddAbove (Φ₄ r '' (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply isCompact_Icc.bddAbove_image
      exact h₂
    have h₄ : BddBelow (Φ₄ r '' (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply isCompact_Icc.bddBelow_image
      exact h₂
    -- Use the boundedness and continuity to conclude the integral is finite
    have h₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;>
      (try simp_all [measurableSet_Icc, Real.volume_Icc])
      <;>
      (try norm_num)
      <;>
      (try assumption)
      <;>
      (try exact h₂)
      <;>
      (try exact isCompact_Icc)
    exact h₅
  -- Use the finite integral to conclude integrability on the interval
  have h₂ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := h₁
  simpa [IntegrableOn, Real.volume_Icc] using h₂

have h₀ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous on [0, 1] to prove integrability
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₂ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
    have h₃ : BddAbove (Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x)) := by
      -- Since Φ₄ is continuous on [0, 1], it attains its maximum
      have h₄ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₅ : ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1), (Φ₄ r) x ≤ M := by
        -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
        have h₆ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
        have h₇ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
        -- Use the extreme value theorem to find the maximum
        have h₈ : ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1), (Φ₄ r) x ≤ M := by
          -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
          have h₉ : ∃ M, ∀ x ∈ (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1), (Φ₄ r) x ≤ M := by
            -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
            exact Metric.exists_bounded_continuousImage h₇ (by
              -- Prove that [0, 1] is a subset of itself
              simpa using subset_univ (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1))
          exact h₉
        exact h₈
      -- Obtain the bound M
      obtain ⟨M, hM⟩ := h₅
      -- Show that M is an upper bound for the range of Φ₄ r
      refine' ⟨M, _⟩
      intro y
      -- Since y is in the range of Φ₄ r, there exists an x such that y = Φ₄ r x
      rcases y with ⟨x, rfl⟩
      -- If x is in [0, 1], then Φ₄ r x ≤ M
      by_cases hx : x ∈ (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)
      · exact hM x hx
      · -- If x is not in [0, 1], then Φ₄ r x is not relevant for the integral
        have h₉ : (Φ₄ r) x ≤ M := by
          -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous and the set is compact
          have h₁₀ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
          -- Since x is not in [0, 1], we can use the fact that Φ₄ r is bounded outside [0, 1]
          -- However, in this case, we can directly use the bound M
          simp_all [Set.mem_Icc]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try linarith) <;>
          (try
            {
              cases' le_or_lt 0 x with hx' hx' <;>
              cases' le_or_lt x 1 with hx'' hx'' <;>
              simp_all [Set.mem_Icc] <;>
              linarith
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              norm_num at *
              <;>
              linarith
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              linarith
            })
        exact h₉
    -- Use the fact that a bounded function on a finite measure set is integrable
    have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous on [0, 1]
      have h₅ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      -- Use the fact that [0, 1] is a compact set
      have h₆ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
      -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
      have h₇ : BddAbove (Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x)) := h₃
      -- Use the fact that a bounded function on a finite measure set is integrable
      have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
        -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous on [0, 1]
        have h₉ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
        -- Use the fact that [0, 1] is a compact set
        have h₁₀ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
        -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is bounded
        have h₁₁ : BddAbove (Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x)) := h₃
        -- Use the fact that a bounded function on a finite measure set is integrable
        apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
        <;> simp_all [measurableSet_Icc]
        <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try linarith) <;>
        (try
          {
            apply ContinuousOn.mono h₉
            <;>
            simp_all [Set.subset_def]
            <;>
            (try norm_num) <;>
            (try linarith)
          })
      exact h₈
    exact h₄
  -- Use the fact that a function with a finite integral is integrable
  have h₁ : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    exact h₀.integrableOn
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₂ : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    apply MeasureTheory.integrableOn_Icc_of_continuous
    exact h₁
  exact h₂

have h₄ : BddAbove ((Φ₄ r) '' Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := h₃.bddAbove
   have h₅ : BddBelow ((Φ₄ r) '' Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := h₃.bddBelow

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on a compact interval to show it is bounded
    have h₃ : BddAbove (Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x)) := by
      -- Since Φ₄ r is continuous on [0,1], it attains its maximum
      have h₄ : IsCompact (Icc 0 1) := isCompact_Icc
      have h₅ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₆ : BddAbove ((Φ₄ r) '' (Icc 0 1)) := by
        apply isCompact_bddAbove_image
        <;> simp_all [continuousOn_iff_continuous_restrict]
        <;> exact h₄
        <;> exact h₅
      -- The range of Φ₄ r is the same as its image on [0,1] because we're restricting to [0,1]
      have h₇ : (Φ₄ r) '' (Icc 0 1) = Set.range (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x) := by
        ext y
        simp [Set.mem_image]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          constructor <;> intro h <;>
          (try { cases' h with x hx }) <;>
          (try { use x <;> aesop }) <;>
          (try { simp_all [Set.mem_Icc] <;> aesop })
        })
      rw [h₇] at h₆
      exact h₆
    -- Use the fact that the function is bounded and the interval has finite measure to show it has finite integral
    have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;> simp_all [continuousOn_iff_continuous_restrict]
      <;> try { exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn }
      <;> try { exact isCompact_Icc }
    exact h₈
  -- Use the fact that the function has a finite integral to conclude it is integrable
  have h₂ : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) volume := by
    exact h₁.integrableOn
  exact h₂

have h₁ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₄ r x) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₂ : ((fun x : ℝ => Φ₄ r x) : ℝ → ℝ) = Φ₄ r := rfl
  have h₃ : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r : ℝ → ℝ) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on a compact interval to deduce integrability
    refine' ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc _ _
    · exact h₁
    · -- Prove that the interval [0, 1] has finite volume
      exact by
        norm_num [Real.volume_Icc]
  simpa [h₂] using h₃

have h₁ : (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ).IsCompact := isCompact_Icc
  have h₂ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => ‖Φ₄ r x‖) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.norm
    exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn.comp (continuousOn_id) (fun x hx => by simp_all)
  have h₃ : BddAbove ( ‖Φ₄ r‖ '' (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) ) := by
    apply h₂.bddAbove'
    <;> simp_all [isCompact_Icc]
  have h₄ : BddBelow ( ‖Φ₄ r‖ '' (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) ) := by
    apply h₂.bddBelow'
    <;> simp_all [isCompact_Icc]
  have h₅ : IntegrableOn (fun x : ℝ => ‖Φ₄ r x‖) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
    <;> (try simp_all)
    <;> (try continuity)
    <;> (try exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn.comp (continuousOn_id) (fun x hx => by simp_all))
  refine' (MeasureTheory.IntegrableOn.of_norm_le h₅ ?_) <;>
    simp_all [Function.comp]
  <;>
  (try norm_num) <;>
  (try linarith)

have h : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₂ : IsCompact (Icc 0 1) := isCompact_Icc
    -- Use the fact that the function is continuous on a compact set to prove integrability
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) :=
      (h₁.hasMeasureIntegralOn volume (by infer_instance)).
        hasFiniteIntegral.mono (by
          apply MeasureTheory.measure_restrict_le _ (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1))
          <;> simp [h₂])
    exact h₃
  exact h

exact h₂

theorem Φ₃_integrableOn {r : ℝ} (hr : r ≥ 0) : IntegrableOn (Φ₃ r)
    (Ioc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
  refine ⟨ContinuousOn.aestronglyMeasurable Φ₃_contDiffOn.continuousOn measurableSet_Ioc, ?_⟩
  sorry

theorem Φ₄_integrableOn {r : ℝ} (hr : r ≥ 0) : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r)
    (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
  refine ⟨ContinuousOn.aestronglyMeasurable Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn measurableSet_Icc, ?_⟩
  sorry

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₂ : (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ) = Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := rfl
    have h₃ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₄ : BddAbove (Set.range fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x) := by
      have h₅ : ∀ x : ℝ, (Φ₄ r) x ≤ 1 := by
        intro x
        have h₆ : (Φ₄ r) x = Real.sqrt (1 - x ^ 2) := by
          simp [Φ₄]
        rw [h₆]
        -- Prove that sqrt(1 - x^2) ≤ 1 for all x
        have h₇ : Real.sqrt (1 - x ^ 2) ≤ 1 := by
          apply Real.sqrt_le_iff.mpr
          constructor
          · nlinarith [sq_nonneg x]
          · nlinarith [sq_nonneg x]
        exact h₇
      -- Since Φ₄ r x ≤ 1 for all x, the set is bounded above by 1
      refine' ⟨1, _⟩
      intro y
      obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := y
      exact h₅ x
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous on [0, 1] and bounded above to show that it has a finite integral
    have h₅ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous on [0, 1] and bounded above to show that it has a finite integral
      have h₆ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Set.Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₇ : BddAbove (Set.range fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x) := h₄
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous on [0, 1] and bounded above to show that it has a finite integral
      have h₈ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
        -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous on [0, 1] and bounded above to show that it has a finite integral
        refine' ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral' (by continuity) _
        -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r is continuous on [0, 1] and bounded above to show that it has a finite integral
        <;> simp_all [Set.Icc_subset_Icc_left]
        <;> norm_num
        <;>
        (try
          {
            use 1
            <;>
            (try
              {
                intro x hx
                simp_all [Φ₄]
                <;>
                (try
                  {
                    have h₉ : Real.sqrt (1 - x ^ 2) ≥ 0 := Real.sqrt_nonneg (1 - x ^ 2)
                    nlinarith
                  })
              })
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            nlinarith
          })
      exact h₈
    -- Convert the integral to the required form
    simpa [h₂] using h₅
  -- Use the fact that Φ₄ r has a finite integral to conclude that it is integrable on [0, 1]
  simpa using h₁.integrable

have h1 : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous on [0,1] to show it has finite integral
    have h2 : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h3 : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
      -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous on [0,1] to show it is integrable
      apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
      exact h2
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is integrable on [0,1] to show it has finite integral
    have h4 : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      rw [hasFiniteIntegral_iff_integrableOn]
      exact h3
    exact h4
  exact h1

have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  -- Use the fact that the interval [0,1] is compact and Φ₄ r is continuous to bound it
  have h₂ : ∃ (C : ℝ), ∀ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |(Φ₄ r x : ℝ)| ≤ C := by
    -- Φ₄ r is continuous on [0,1], which is compact, hence it is bounded
    have h₃ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₄ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
      exact ContinuousOn.comp (continuousOn_id) (by continuity) (by tauto)
    -- Use the extreme value theorem to find the bound
    obtain ⟨x₀, hx₀, hx₀'⟩ := IsCompact.exists_isMinOn (h₃) (h₄.isClosed) (Set.nonempty_Icc.mpr (by norm_num))
    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁, hx₁'⟩ := IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn (h₃) (h₄.isClosed) (Set.nonempty_Icc.mpr (by norm_num))
    use max (|(Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)|) (|(Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)|)
    intro x hx
    have h₅ : (Φ₄ r x : ℝ) ≤ (Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ) := by
      apply hx₁'
      exact hx
    have h₆ : (Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ) ≤ (Φ₄ r x : ℝ) := by
      apply hx₀'
      exact hx
    have h₇ : (Φ₄ r x : ℝ) ≥ (Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ) := by linarith
    have h₈ : (Φ₄ r x : ℝ) ≤ (Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ) := by linarith
    have h₉ : |(Φ₄ r x : ℝ)| ≤ max (|(Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)|) (|(Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)|) := by
      have h₁₀ : |(Φ₄ r x : ℝ)| ≤ max (|(Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)|) (|(Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)|) := by
        cases' le_total (|(Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)|) (|(Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)|) with h h <;> simp_all [max_eq_right, max_eq_left] <;>
          (try { contradiction }) <;>
          (try {
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) with h₁₁ h₁₁ <;>
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)) with h₁₂ h₁₂ <;>
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)) with h₁₃ h₁₃ <;>
            nlinarith
          }) <;>
          (try {
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) with h₁₁ h₁₁ <;>
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x₀ : ℝ)) with h₁₂ h₁₂ <;>
            cases' abs_cases ((Φ₄ r x₁ : ℝ)) with h₁₃ h₁₃ <;>
            nlinarith
          })
      exact h₁₀
    exact h₉
  -- Use the bound to show the integral is finite
  obtain ⟨C, hC⟩ := h₂
  have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₄ : ∀ (x : ℝ), x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 → |(Φ₄ r x : ℝ)| ≤ C := hC
    have h₅ : StronglyMeasurable (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) := by
      -- Prove that Φ₄ r is strongly measurable (continuous implies measurable)
      have h₅₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      have h₅₂ : StronglyMeasurable (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the restriction to the compact interval is continuous and thus measurable
        apply Continuous.aestronglyMeasurable
        exact by continuity
      exact h₅₂
    -- Use the fact that the function is bounded to show the integral is finite
    have h₆ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r x : ℝ)) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      refine' (HasFiniteIntegral.of_abs _).of_norm
      -- Use the fact that the absolute value is bounded by a constant
      refine' (hasFiniteIntegral_of_ae_stronglyMeasurable (by
        -- Show that the function is strongly measurable
        exact StronglyMeasurable.aestronglyMeasurable h₅) (by
        -- Show that the function is almost everywhere bounded
        filter_upwards [ae_restrict_mem measurableSet_Icc] with x hx
        have h₇ : x ∈ Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 := hx
        have h₈ : |(Φ₄ r x : ℝ)| ≤ C := h₄ x h₇
        exact by
          simpa [Real.norm_eq_abs] using h₈) (by
        -- Show that the bound is integrable
        exact hasFiniteIntegral_const (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) (C : ℝ)))
    -- Cast the integral back to the original type
    have h₇ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      simpa [Complex.norm_eq_abs] using h₆
    exact h₇
  -- Use the fact that the integral is finite to conclude the proof
  exact h₃

have h : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous and the interval is compact to deduce integrability
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₂ : VolumeMeasurable (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ) := by
      apply measurableSet_Icc
    -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact sets are integrable
    refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuousOn_of_lebesgue_ae_measure_ne_zero _ _ (by
      -- Prove that the Lebesgue measure of the interval is not zero
      have h₃ : (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ)) ≠ 0 := by
        -- The interval [0,1] has positive measure
        have h₄ : (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ)) (Icc 0 1 : Set ℝ) = 1 := by
          simp [intervalIntegral.volume_Icc]
        -- If the measure were zero, it would contradict h₄
        intro h₅
        rw [h₅] at h₄
        norm_num at h₄
      -- Use the fact that the measure is not zero
      simpa [h₃] using h₃)
    · -- Prove the function is bounded
      exact IsCompact.exists_isCompact_closure (isCompact_Icc : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1))
    · -- Prove the interval is measurable
      exact h₂
  exact h
integrable

refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_hasFiniteIntegral_restrict _
  -- The integral of Φ₄ r over [0,1] is bounded because the function is continuous and the interval is compact.
  -- Since Φ₄ r is continuous on [0,1], it attains its maximum and minimum, and thus the integral is finite.
  have h₁ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => (Φ₄ r) x) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  -- Use the fact that continuous functions on compact sets are bounded to show that the integral is finite.
  refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuous_on_of_ae_stronglyMeasurable (by
    -- Prove that the function is continuous on the interval [0,1].
    exact h₁) (by
    -- Prove that the function is almost everywhere strongly measurable.
    exact ContinuousOn.aestronglyMeasurable h₁ measurableSet_Icc)
  <;> simp_all [measurableSet_Icc]
  <;> try norm_num
  <;> try linarith

<;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try refine' hasFiniteIntegral_of_le (fun x hx => _) ⟨0, _⟩) <;>
    (try simp_all [Φ₁, Φ₂, Φ₃, Φ₄, J₁, J₂, J₃, J₄, J₅, J₆]) <;>
    (try norm_num at hx ⊢) <;>
    (try linarith) <;>
    (try
      {
        norm_num [Complex.abs, Complex.normSq, pow_two, pow_three] at *
        <;>
        ring_nf at *
        <;>
        positivity
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        apply le_of_sub_nonneg
        <;>
        field_simp at *
        <;>
        ring_nf at *
        <;>
        positivity
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        positivity
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
        <;>
        exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
      })
h₂

have h : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
    have h₁ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc 0 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    have h₂ : IsCompact (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := isCompact_Icc
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc 0 1)) := by
      -- Use the fact that a continuous function on a compact set is integrable
      apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
      <;> simp_all [h₁, h₂.measurableSet]
      <;> try norm_num
      <;> try linarith
    exact h₃
  exact h

have h₁ : (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1 : Set ℝ).IsCompact := isCompact_Icc
  have h₂ : ContinuousOn (fun x : ℝ => Φ₄ r x) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  have h₃ : IsContinuousOnWith (fun x : ℝ => Φ₄ r x) (NormedAddCommGroup ℝ) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := by
    infer_instance
  have h₄ : HasFiniteIntegral (fun x : ℝ => Φ₄ r x) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    apply ContinuousOn.hasFiniteIntegral
    <;> simp_all [h₁, h₂]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  exact h₄.isIntegrable

have h₁ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
    have h₂ : ContinuousOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) := Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
    -- Use the fact that Φ₄ is continuous and the interval is compact to show the integral is finite
    have h₃ : HasFiniteIntegral (Φ₄ r) (volume.restrict (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1)) := by
      apply hasFiniteIntegral_of_continuousOn
      <;>
      (try simp_all) <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try continuity) <;>
      (try aesop)
    exact h₃
  exact h₁
successful_attempts:
have h : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
    exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  exact h.hasFiniteIntegral

have h : IntegrableOn (Φ₄ r) (Icc (0 : ℝ) 1) volume := by
    apply ContinuousOn.integrableOn_Icc
    <;> exact Φ₄_contDiffOn.continuousOn
  exact h.2
